mnemonics.title: DDA Steps Memory
mnemonics.content: Remember: CALCULATE SLOPE → DETERMINE DIRECTION → ITERATE WITH INCREMENTS → ROUND TO INTEGERS → PLOT PIXELS

mnemonics.title: Slope Decision Rule
mnemonics.content: '|m| < 1' → 'X is Primary' (step in x, y+=m); '|m| > 1' → 'Y is Primary' (step in y, x+=1/m)

mnemonics.title: DDA Advantages
mnemonics.content: SIMPLE - SImple to iMplement, PRecise for short lines, LEarns quickly (SIMPLE)

mnemonics.title: DDA Disadvantages
mnemonics.content: FLOATING - Floating-point Operations, Lining errors accumulate, Accumulated errors, TTruncation/rounding, Inefficient compared to Integer methods, Generates drift over long lines, Expensive computation (FLOATING)

acronyms.usage: Line drawing algorithm in raster graphics
Determines step direction in DDA algorithm

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a hill: slope 0 = flat road, slope 1 = 45° incline, slope >1 = steep cliff, slope <1 = gentle slope
Draw a coordinate grid; for |m|<1 the line is more horizontal, so step horizontally; for |m|>1 the line is more vertical, so step vertically
Think of pixel positions like rounding to nearest whole number on a number line

analogies.analogy: Like walking along a path: if the path is gentle (|m|<1), you take equal x-steps and small y-steps; if steep (|m|>1), you take equal y-steps and small x-steps
Like measuring with a slightly imprecise ruler - small errors add up over longer distances
Like placing tiles along a line - you choose the nearest tile position at each step
